item.title: Native Voices
item.description: Long before digital preservation or cultural funding, two women quietly reshaped the course of Native American history. Ethnomusicologist Frances Densmore, working in the early 20th century, recorded more than 2,000 tribal songs on wax cylinders using rudimentary phonograph technology. Her work documented ceremonial chants, healing songs, and oral histories from dozens of tribes—many of which were at risk of being lost to colonization and cultural suppression. Across decades, another guardian emerged: Gladys Tantaquidgeon, a Mohegan elder and traditional medicine woman. She chronicled her tribe’s ancient remedies and beliefs in handwritten notebooks. Though stored away for years, these records proved crucial when the Mohegan Tribe sought federal recognition in 1994—a process requiring deep proof of continuous cultural identity. Her documentation helped restore sovereignty and inspired a cultural renaissance marked by the founding of the Mohegan Sun. These acts of preservation were more than academic—they were acts of resistance. In a world that sought to erase Indigenous cultures, these women safeguarded what mattered most: language, ceremony, and memory. Their legacy remains a cornerstone of historical preservation and Indigenous resilience.
item.dc:creator: TheTaoofRob

item.title: The Unbearable Lightness of Being Frank
item.description:
Got home, Frank started telling me about his day
Some of you have asked how Chessie and Puddles are doing with Frank. Well....
There are bad days
And there are good days.
Honestly, Puddles is really chill with Frank and actually tried to groom Frank for a little bit. Frank was not okay with that though. But sitting near each other is okay. Chessie is going to take some time to warm up to him. That's okay. It's only been five days.
Frank is Frank.
Bonus video. @KittensForDays reminded me that I have a Ring setup in my house as well. Here's Frank having a blast bouncing off the furniture. Going to see Superman tonight. It should be good, I've heard good things about it. Hope your Thursday evening is going well.
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten

item.title: Appreciating your parents afterwards ....
item.description:
Good parents love to make their kid's lunches ...
More weird than funny to me. How many times did this happen?
That is so Dad.
Amen to this. And the taking of pictures along the way. Memories for a lifetime.
Yeah ... that is a weird flex if you ask me.
Yep, I know that feeling. So great to be entrusted with your kids' friends and making sure they get home safely. The other parents certainly appreciate you.

item.title: I have always wanted to make my own single-line shirt designs so today I have begun my quest.
item.description: This is my first concept. The design itself is one non-intersecting line. That will be my main theme. Wish me luck! Once I start committing these concepts into reality, I will host them on my new IG account. Even if you never want to get my designs, any support to my account would be appreciated https://www.instagram.com/tee.foust?igsh=bjRqcTUxMHh2eGpz

item.title: Five Nights of Frank
item.description:
I showed you my toe beans. Please respond.
Frank. That's grown up food. Your kitten food is in the cupboard.
Sleepy Frank pose 1
Sleepy Frank pose 2
Sleepy Frank pose wtf.
Bonus minute of Frank doin the groomin. Good night everyone. It's almost the weekend. Hang in there. Didn't get to see Superman tonight. Ah well, we'll go Sunday.

DOOOKEY!!!
Two things that never fail to crack me up are jokes about geese and jokes about mambo no. 5
I had this stuck in my head for like two weeks once. Art for the sake of art, honestly.
Jokes about shatzenpants too.
item.dc:creator: stonetemplefox05

item.title: A Lot of Dogs, Probably; a Fifty-eighth Dump
item.description:
Why does eighth look like that
The SATISFACTION.
We used to own a fn island! Now I own a blanket and three used couches (trying to throw away the old dog couch but my potato spine only lets me lift 15lbs)
Definitely something Iggy should do, but she loves everyone.
Borat voice: my life.
I want to become a tree when I die. Not an ash, though. My area has a big problem with emerald ash borers.
I was a double accident: my mom was told she couldn't conceive any more children, and then they had my parents excited for a son but I came out instead.
Unless it's preordering fast food, I need a big screen and buttons.
(I'm not actually dehydrated, I drink 3-4L of water per day, and I just sit on the couch in the AC)
Going to make these for all the parents this Xmas.
Currently fighting for my life in the washroom instead of walking Iggy.
My partner just saw a cassowary for the first time and thought it was AI lol
Sugar!
There are too many whimsical purses, and I want them all.
My partner is always making fun of me and my four beverages.
Butter and Iggy.
I wish my partner would fence with me. I'd kick his butt, even with potato spine and garbage leg.
I have a picture of my brother doing this face and it makes me laugh every time I see it.
One died of ass cancer! Wasn't as satisfying as I had hoped it'd be though.
I feel like Bis wasn't around as long as they should've been.
Ever since going on medication for acid reflux, I have been testing my luck with coffee and hot sauce. One day, I will find the limit.
I just ate a bag of the crunchy ones yesterday and my cuticles are still orange. I have washed my hands no fewer than twenty times since.
Relatable.
Tacos and dogs.
Iggy tax. Can't remember if I posted this one already.
item.dc:creator: AshesVonDust

item.title: GovBrief Today - What Happened #161
item.description: The pushback is becoming a roar and it's making them panic. Medical clinic staff grabbed ICE agents' hands and yelled "get off him." California protesters stared down tear gas and rubber bullets for farmworkers in unorganized protests that spread by word-of-mouth. A whistleblower dropped receipts proving Trump's judge pick told his staff to tell courts to "go fuck themselves." Florida lawmakers sued DeSantis for his detention camp access. When people see others refuse to back down, they stop backing down too. The regime's desperate polygraph tests prove they can feel us getting stronger. Keep pushing. We're reinforcing each other, and it's working. Don't rely on the algorithm to keep you informed. Let me send this to you every night, free and private, so that you always know what's going on. Add your email at govbrief dot today, on Facebook, or on Substack at Gov Brief Today. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com 1. https://go.govbrief.today/camarillo-protest-carbajal 2. https://go.govbrief.today/ice-detains-man-surgery-center 3. https://go.govbrief.today/35-percent-canadian-tariffs 4. https://go.govbrief.today/adams-malaysia-ambassador 5. https://go.govbrief.today/dhs-rules-slow-flood-relief 6. https://go.govbrief.today/un-warns-millions-die 7. https://go.govbrief.today/gunmakers-lose-appeal 8. https://go.govbrief.today/judge-block-birthright-ban 9. https://go.govbrief.today/fbi-polygraphs-criticize-patel 10. https://go.govbrief.today/defense-buys-stake-rare-earths-company 11. https://go.govbrief.today/tmobile-ends-dei 12. https://go.govbrief.today/whistleblower-text-bove 13. https://go.govbrief.today/missouri-repeals-2024-voter-mandate 14. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-sexual-assault-verdict-upheld 15. https://go.govbrief.today/lawmakers-sue-desantis-access-camp 16. https://go.govbrief.today/ice-may-deport-abrego-agarcia-again 17. https://go.govbrief.today/headstart-immigrants-banned 18. https://go.govbrief.today/gmu-dei-investigation 19. https://go.govbrief.today/state-dept-layoffs-coming 20. https://go.govbrief.today/ssa-phone-delays 21. https://go.govbrief.today/protest-guides-revealed 22. https://go.govbrief.today/trump-aides-trading Yesterday's Top Story - https://go.govbrief.today/trump-not-think-pause Fighting Back - https://go.govbrief.today/khalil-sues-20million Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.description: Strike for freedom!
item.dc:creator: AgentKGB

item.title: The results of the X-ray are in!!
item.description: Bestie and I took mama to the vet today for an X-ray to see how many babies she’s carrying! And bestie and I learned two very important things: 1 - A severe UTI in a cat can have the same signs and symptoms as going into labor (discharge, waddling, excessive licking, even “contractions”) And… 2 - Mama is NOT PREGNANT. I repeat. MAMA IS NOT PREGNANT. I just…I just can’t. She had us all fooled. Bestie and I just stared at each other for like a minute. We’re flabbergasted. They gave mama (I mean I guess I can’t call her that anymore, so we’ll go with the name the humane society gave her which is Waffles) an antibiotic shot for the UTI and sent us on our merry way. I don’t even know what to say. I’m sorry I got you all on board with this “pregnancy.” I feel like a freaking idiot. I’ll keep her until Monday so the antibiotics have a chance to work and then I’ll take her back. And now I’m going to make a stiff drink and try not to focus on how thoroughly and completely I fucked this up
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: Pixie & Brutus: Come
item.description:
Today's Tiabeanie Tax; Bean cuddling with Dad's cappybara while she sleeps
Bonus Tax! This is my roommate's very elusive cat, Spitfire. She's a very shy rescue, about 14 years old now. She doesn't come out very much and is most grumpy that Uncle has brought a Bean into our home. New baby was not approved of! A bit of bonus cat tax as a huge thanks to @ParallelParkingInABurka for their help and generosity
item.dc:creator: TheUnnamedPoet
